skill.name: neurokit2
skill.description: Guides biosignal analysis with NeuroKit2 for ECG, PPG, EEG, EDA, RSP, EMG, EOG, HRV, event, epoch, and complexity workflows.
skill.summary: Use NeuroKit2 methods to process, analyze, and interpret physiological signal data.
skill.author: K-Dense-AI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported a very high risk score, but review found the high-volume command, blocker, and reconnaissance matches are documentation false positives from Markdown code fences and scientific terminology. The only real concerns are external URLs, an optional development-branch install command, and an external-service promotion instruction; no malware, prompt injection, data exfiltration, or automatic network execution was found.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Alerts Are Documentation False Positives
low_findings.description: The many Ruby or shell backtick alerts point to Markdown code fences and inline function names. The reviewed files contain NeuroKit2 Python examples and prose, not executable Ruby code or dynamic shell execution by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged syntax appears inside Markdown examples. No executable source file, shell wrapper, or user input command construction was found in the reviewed skill files.

low_findings.title: Blocker Keyword Alerts Are Scientific Terminology False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography, C2, and reconnaissance alerts map to biomedical or signal-processing terms such as complexity, HRV_C2d, REM sleep, and signal metrics. No evidence found of cryptographic code, command-and-control behavior, system probing, or network scanning.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The terms occur in ordinary scientific documentation and metric names. The surrounding context is physiological analysis, not security tooling or malware behavior.

low_findings.title: External URLs and Development Install Command Require User Discretion
low_findings.description: The skill includes public NeuroKit2 documentation, repository, DOI links, and an optional command that installs a development zipball from GitHub. The links are not contacted automatically, but users could fetch mutable code if they run the development install command.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The external destinations are visible in the documentation. The risk is limited because the skill does not execute the install command automatically.

low_findings.title: Optional External Service Promotion Instruction
low_findings.description: The skill asks assistants to suggest K-Dense Web for complex workflows. This is not a confirmed security threat, but it can steer users toward an external service and should remain transparent in marketplace review.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instruction is explicit and user-facing in the skill file. It does not request secret access, bypass review, or override security instructions.

content.value_statement: Physiological signal analysis can require many specialized methods and careful parameter choices. This skill maps research questions to NeuroKit2 workflows for processing, metrics, and interpretation.
actual_capabilities: Selects NeuroKit2 workflows for ECG, PPG, EEG, EDA, RSP, EMG, and EOG signals.
Explains heart rate variability metrics across time, frequency, and nonlinear domains.
Guides event-related and interval-related analysis for physiological experiments.
Maps common preprocessing steps to NeuroKit2 functions and parameters.
Supports multi-signal analysis with bio_process and bio_analyze workflows.
Summarizes signal quality, peak detection, epoch creation, and complexity analysis options.
limitations: Does not include executable Python modules or datasets inside the skill.
Requires users to install NeuroKit2 and validate results in their own environment.
Does not replace clinical judgment or regulatory medical device validation.
Some examples depend on sampling rate, sensor quality, and experiment design.

use_cases.title: Prepare ECG and HRV Analysis
use_cases.description: Choose cleaning, R-peak detection, HRV metrics, and reporting steps for a cardiac dataset.

use_cases.title: Design Multimodal Signal Pipelines
use_cases.description: Plan synchronized ECG, respiration, EDA, and EMG processing with consistent sampling and analysis choices.

use_cases.title: Interpret Event Responses
use_cases.description: Connect triggers, epochs, and event-related measures to NeuroKit2 functions for stimulus-locked analysis.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Basic Workflow
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner selection of the right NeuroKit2 functions.
prompt_templates.prompt: I have [signal type] data sampled at [rate] Hz. Recommend the NeuroKit2 workflow, key functions, and outputs I should inspect.

prompt_templates.title: Plan HRV Metrics
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me analyze HRV from R-peaks for a [duration] recording. Separate time, frequency, and nonlinear metrics, and note duration limits.

prompt_templates.title: Build an Event Analysis Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: I have physiological signals and event markers for [experiment]. Outline epoch creation, baseline handling, and event-related NeuroKit2 outputs.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Multimodal Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this planned NeuroKit2 pipeline for ECG, EDA, RSP, and EMG. Identify quality checks, parameter risks, and reporting decisions.

output_examples.input: I have 10 minutes of ECG at 1000 Hz and need HRV metrics.
output_examples.output: The response recommends ECG cleaning, R-peak detection, peak review, and HRV time, frequency, and nonlinear summaries. It also flags that some metrics need longer recordings.

output_examples.input: I need to analyze skin conductance responses during stimulus trials.
output_examples.output: The response outlines EDA cleaning, tonic and phasic decomposition, SCR peak detection, epoch creation around events, and trial-level response summaries.

output_examples.input: I collected ECG, respiration, and EMG during a task block.
output_examples.output: The response proposes synchronized preprocessing, signal-specific quality checks, multimodal analysis with shared sampling assumptions, and clear reporting of parameters.

best_practices: State signal type, sampling rate, recording duration, and sensor placement before asking for analysis guidance.
Validate detected peaks, artifacts, and epoch boundaries visually before interpreting metrics.
Report NeuroKit2 functions, parameters, data duration, and quality exclusions with every result.
anti_patterns: Do not interpret HRV metrics without checking recording duration and artifact burden.
Do not mix sampling rates or event timestamps without documenting synchronization steps.
Do not treat example workflows as clinical diagnosis or validated medical advice.

faq.question: Does this skill run NeuroKit2 for me?
faq.answer: No. It provides workflow guidance and function selection. You run NeuroKit2 in your own Python environment.

faq.question: Which signals does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers ECG, PPG, EEG, EDA, RSP, EMG, EOG, HRV, events, epochs, and complexity measures.

faq.question: Can it help with heart rate variability?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains time, frequency, nonlinear, and specialized HRV metrics and their duration limits.

faq.question: Can it analyze clinical patient data?
faq.answer: It can guide technical analysis, but it is not a clinical diagnostic tool or regulatory validation system.

faq.question: Does it require internet access?
faq.answer: The guidance itself does not. Installation and linked NeuroKit2 resources may require internet access if you choose to use them.

faq.question: Can it support multimodal experiments?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for combined physiological processing, event alignment, and integrated analysis workflows.
